title: Full-text description search regression (~10-15s) after v2.3 update
problem: Searching across task descriptions takes 10-15 seconds, while searching by task title remains fast. The reporter has a large number of tasks and noticed the slowdown approximately 2 weeks ago, around the time they updated to v2.3.
root_cause_hypothesis: The v2.3 update likely introduced a regression in how full-text description search is executed — possibilities include a dropped or rebuilt full-text index on the description field, a query change that bypasses the index (e.g., switching from indexed search to LIKE/ILIKE scan), or a new feature (e.g., searching attachments or comments) that broadened the search scope without corresponding index support.
reproduction_steps: Create or use an account with a large number of tasks (hundreds+) that have non-trivial descriptions
Perform a keyword search using full-text/description search mode
Compare with a title-only search on the same keyword — expect near-instant results
environment: TaskFlow v2.3, work laptop (OS/specs not specified but unlikely to be the bottleneck given title search is fast)
impact: Users with large task collections experience unusable search latency on full-text description searches. Title-only search is unaffected. This degrades a core workflow for power users.
recommended_fix: Diff the search query path between v2.2 and v2.3, focusing on how description search is executed. Check whether a full-text index on the description column exists and is being used (run EXPLAIN/ANALYZE on the query). If the index was dropped or the query changed to a sequential scan, restore indexed search. Also check if v2.3 expanded the search scope (e.g., now including comments or attachments) without adding indexes.
proposed_test_case: Performance test: seed a database with 1,000+ tasks with multi-paragraph descriptions. Assert that a full-text keyword search completes in under 1 second. Run this test against both v2.2 (baseline) and v2.3 to confirm the regression and verify the fix.
information_gaps: Exact number of tasks in the reporter's account (unlikely to change the fix direction)
Whether the slowdown started immediately after the v2.3 update or a few days later (could indicate a background migration or index rebuild issue)
